page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the instance utilizes a dedicated Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. This hardware configuration consists of 1 physical core supporting 2 threads, backed by 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 33 MB L3 cache. The CPU features advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance is optimized for memory-intensive workloads rather than raw compute power. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is rated as average, and single-threaded gzip compression achieves an average throughput of 23.53 GB/sec. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the lower percentiles, with read-only large block memory bandwidth at 13.86 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 11.77 GB/sec. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET performance are relatively low, with Redis handling 1.67 million operations per second.

The n2-highmem-2 does not include bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external block storage configuration. Given its high memory density relative to its low vCPU count, this instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require a larger memory footprint without demanding high parallel processing capabilities. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, memory-bound utility scripts, and lightweight web serving applications that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and a high RAM-to-core ratio.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, providing a high ratio of 8 GB of memory per core. Powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor on the x86_64 architecture, the instance includes 1 physical core with 2 threads, supported by a 33 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. While single-core performance and gzip compression benchmarks are average, multi-core processing and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively low, with large-block memory read speeds at 13.86 GB/sec. The instance does not include bundled local storage or public IPv4 addresses. It offers a cost-effective profile for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute power, making it highly suitable for small databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive utility applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon CPU. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine offering a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads requiring dedicated Intel Xeon processing.
bullet_points: Dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 2 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core for memory-bound tasks
Supports AVX-512 instruction sets for vector and cryptographic operations
No bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Best suited for small databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive utility scripts
